refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (local vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the mechanisms that enable it, making it verifiable and decomposable. The refined FR explicitly acknowledges both on-site and tele-operated modes and ties productivity to a measurable, time-bounded evaluation context.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials (e.g., standardized earthmoving scenarios) with both on-site and remote operators; measure actual throughput (m³/h or ton/h) against the customer-defined productivity_target; log task completion times, material moved, and safety incidents; compare against baseline or competitor equipment.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical efficiency and control responsive to operator commands.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation subsystem shall transmit operator intent (commands) to the vehicle and return real-time telemetry (position, load, status) with latency and fidelity sufficient for safe, productive remote operation.

FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety policies (geofencing, speed limits, collision avoidance, emergency stop) and alert operators and site managers to hazardous conditions.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall provide measurement and reporting of productivity metrics (throughput, cycle time, utilization) to enable verification against productivity_target.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-power hydraulic earthmoving vehicle with real-time teleoperation and safety monitoring, optimized for task cycle time and operator responsiveness.
DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle platform with swappable attachments, coupled with a tele-operator interface and autonomous safety enforcement layer, to maximize task flexibility and throughput.

DP-C: Implement a hybrid on-site/remote operation architecture where the vehicle operates autonomously for routine tasks (e.g., repetitive digging) and switches to tele-operation for complex or safety-critical maneuvers, with continuous safety monitoring.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Integrate a high-power hydraulic earthmoving vehicle with real-time teleoperation and safety monitoring, optimized for task cycle time and operator responsiveness.

potential_sources: Vehicle mechanical performance (cycle time, load capacity) is independent of teleoperation latency and safety policy enforcement; however, operator responsiveness depends on telemetry fidelity and network reliability.
Safety enforcement (geofencing, speed limits) must be coordinated with vehicle control authority to avoid conflicts (e.g., safety override vs. operator command).
Productivity measurement depends on accurate telemetry and task logging, which are coupled to both vehicle sensors and teleoperation network.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits acceptable decoupling at the functional level: vehicle design, teleoperation, and safety are logically separable. However, integration points (control authority, telemetry routing, emergency stop) require careful interface definition to avoid runtime coupling. DP-B and DP-C introduce additional complexity (modularity, autonomy) that may increase coupling risk and development cost; DP-A is selected for clarity and risk reduction at this stage.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Operator skill and training are sufficient to achieve productivity_target with responsive tele-operation; risk: inadequate operator training or interface design could limit throughput.
Assumption: Network infrastructure (latency, bandwidth, reliability) is adequate for real-time teleoperation; risk: poor network conditions could degrade operator responsiveness and safety.

Assumption: Hydraulic vehicle technology is mature and can be integrated with teleoperation without major redesign; risk: integration complexity (sensors, control electronics) could delay development.

Assumption: Worksite safety policies are well-defined and can be encoded as geofencing, speed limits, and collision avoidance rules; risk: ambiguous or conflicting policies could lead to safety enforcement failures.
Assumption: Productivity_target is achievable with current earthmoving technology and operator skill; risk: target may be unrealistic, requiring design iteration or scope change.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-A to explicitly specify: (1) vehicle control architecture (e.g., proportional hydraulic valves with electronic feedback), (2) teleoperation latency budget (e.g., <200 ms round-trip), (3) safety enforcement mechanism (e.g., autonomous geofencing layer with operator override capability), and (4) telemetry data model (e.g., position, load, engine status, safety flags). This will reduce ambiguity and support detailed design and IM assignment.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration module that orchestrates the Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems to deliver the productivity KPI. It owns the overall system architecture, integration interfaces, and end-to-end performance verification.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Site Manager or Operator provides task definitions (e.g., dig zone, load target, dump location) and productivity targets to the NGHE Solution; NGHE Solution parses and distributes task parameters to Vehicle and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution aggregates telemetry from Vehicle (position, load, engine status) and Safety Services (safety state, alerts) and reports productivity metrics (throughput, cycle time, utilization) to Site Manager and Maintenance systems.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution broadcasts safety alerts (geofence breach, collision risk, emergency stop) to Operator, Tele-operator, and Safety Manager for situational awareness and incident response.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution maintains network connectivity for teleoperation (command/telemetry exchange), safety monitoring, and productivity reporting; supports both on-site (local network) and remote (WAN) operation modes.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for the entire productivity KPI requirement. It owns system integration, end-to-end performance, and interface orchestration. Minimal diffusion: only the Network Link interface may be shared with external infrastructure (not owned by NGHE).

Vehicle owns mechanical task execution (dig, load, transport, dump) and is a critical component of productivity. However, Vehicle alone cannot achieve the productivity KPI without teleoperation and safety services; productivity measurement and reporting are diffused across NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

Teleoperation enables remote operator control and real-time telemetry, which are necessary for productivity but not sufficient. Productivity depends on vehicle performance, safety constraints, and task definition; Teleoperation is a critical enabler but not the sole owner of the KPI.

Safety Services enforces safety policies and alerts, which constrain productivity (e.g., speed limits, geofencing) but do not directly drive it. Safety is a prerequisite for safe productivity but is diffused across Vehicle (mechanical safety), Teleoperation (operator safety), and Safety Services (policy enforcement).

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner of the productivity KPI requirement because it integrates all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and is responsible for end-to-end performance verification. The requirement cannot be satisfied by any single subsystem in isolation; it requires coordinated execution across all three. NGHE Solution owns the system architecture, integration interfaces, and productivity measurement/reporting.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'catch-all' module that diffuses responsibility for productivity across subsystems without clear ownership of specific performance drivers (e.g., vehicle cycle time, teleoperation latency, safety overhead). Mitigation: Define clear performance budgets for each subsystem (e.g., Vehicle: 80% of productivity_target, Teleoperation: <5% latency overhead, Safety Services: <10% throughput penalty).

Risk: Productivity measurement and reporting may be implemented inconsistently across subsystems, leading to inaccurate KPI tracking. Mitigation: NGHE Solution owns the telemetry aggregation and productivity calculation logic; subsystems provide standardized telemetry feeds.

Risk: Integration testing and verification of the productivity KPI may be deferred to late stages, increasing risk of unmet targets. Mitigation: Establish early integration test plans and productivity benchmarks at each subsystem level.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine the NGHE Solution IM boundary to explicitly define: (1) the system integration architecture (e.g., central controller, message bus, or distributed control), (2) the telemetry aggregation and productivity calculation logic, (3) the performance budgets for each subsystem (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services), and (4) the verification and validation strategy for the productivity KPI. This will clarify ownership and reduce integration risk.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR clearly specifies the productivity outcome (≥ productivity_target, m³/h or ton/h) and the context (representative operations, evaluation period, safety compliance). DP-A (integrated vehicle with teleoperation and safety monitoring) logically addresses the FR by combining mechanical task execution, remote operator control, and safety enforcement. The mapping is sound: vehicle performance drives throughput, teleoperation enables remote operation, and safety services ensure compliance. No functional gaps or contradictions identified.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Productivity measurement logic is distributed across Vehicle (task execution), Teleoperation (telemetry transmission), and Safety Services (safety overhead calculation). Mitigation: NGHE Solution owns the aggregation and calculation; subsystems provide standardized telemetry feeds.

Diffusion Risk 2: Control authority and emergency stop are shared between Vehicle (mechanical control) and Safety Services (policy enforcement). Mitigation: Define clear control hierarchy and override rules (e.g., safety override takes precedence).

Diffusion Risk 3: Network Link interface is external to NGHE Solution and may be owned by site infrastructure. Mitigation: Define network SLAs (latency, bandwidth, availability) as part of system requirements.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for the productivity KPI. It owns system integration, end-to-end performance, and interface orchestration. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are contained subsystems that contribute to the KPI but do not own it individually. The IM assignment is clear and reduces diffusion risk by centralizing productivity measurement and reporting in NGHE Solution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: The refined FR is verifiable through representative operations trials: measure actual throughput (m³/h or ton/h) against productivity_target, log task completion times and material moved, and compare against baseline or competitor equipment. Verification requires instrumentation of the Vehicle (sensors for position, load, cycle time) and Teleoperation (latency, command/telemetry logging). Safety compliance can be verified through incident logs and policy enforcement audits.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the productivity KPI to NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-FRs are allocated to Vehicle (task execution), Teleoperation (remote operation), Safety Services (safety enforcement), and NGHE Solution (measurement and reporting). Each sub-FR is traceable to a specific subsystem and can be verified independently and as part of the integrated system.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Define productivity_target as a specific, measurable value (e.g., 150 m³/h for earthmoving, 50 ton/h for loading) and specify the evaluation period (e.g., 8-hour workday, 5-day trial). This will enable objective verification.

Revision 2: Specify the representative operations scenarios (e.g., standard dig-load-dump cycle, varying terrain, weather conditions) to ensure reproducibility and fairness of productivity measurement.
Revision 3: Define the safety compliance criteria (e.g., zero safety incidents, 100% geofence enforcement, <5% false positive alerts) to ensure that productivity is achieved safely.
Revision 4: Establish performance budgets for each subsystem (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) to guide detailed design and integration testing.

decision.justification: The refined FR is clear, verifiable, and well-decomposed into sub-FRs. The selected DP (integrated vehicle with teleoperation and safety monitoring) is sound and logically addresses the FR. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is appropriate and reduces diffusion risk. However, the sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) require further decomposition to guide detailed design and integration. Specifically, the Vehicle subsystem (FR-NGHE-001.1) needs to be decomposed into mechanical and control design parameters; the Teleoperation subsystem (FR-NGHE-001.2) needs to specify latency, bandwidth, and operator interface requirements; the Safety Services subsystem (FR-NGHE-001.3) needs to define specific safety policies and enforcement mechanisms; and the measurement subsystem (FR-NGHE-001.4) needs to specify telemetry data models and productivity calculation logic. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will clarify these details and reduce integration risk.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical efficiency and control responsive to operator commands.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation subsystem shall transmit operator intent (commands) to the vehicle and return real-time telemetry (position, load, status) with latency and fidelity sufficient for safe, productive remote operation.

FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety policies (geofencing, speed limits, collision avoidance, emergency stop) and alert operators and site managers to hazardous conditions.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall provide measurement and reporting of productivity metrics (throughput, cycle time, utilization) to enable verification against productivity_target.

assumptions: Operator skill and training are sufficient to achieve productivity_target with responsive tele-operation.
Network infrastructure (latency, bandwidth, reliability) is adequate for real-time teleoperation.
Hydraulic vehicle technology is mature and can be integrated with teleoperation without major redesign.
Worksite safety policies are well-defined and can be encoded as geofencing, speed limits, and collision avoidance rules.
Productivity_target is achievable with current earthmoving technology and operator skill.
Representative operations scenarios are well-defined and reproducible for verification.
open_risks: Risk: Inadequate operator training or interface design could limit throughput below productivity_target.
Risk: Poor network conditions could degrade operator responsiveness and safety.
Risk: Integration complexity (sensors, control electronics) could delay development or increase cost.
Risk: Ambiguous or conflicting safety policies could lead to safety enforcement failures.
Risk: Productivity_target may be unrealistic, requiring design iteration or scope change.
Risk: Productivity measurement logic may be inconsistent across subsystems, leading to inaccurate KPI tracking.
Risk: Integration testing and verification of the productivity KPI may be deferred to late stages.
evidence_needed: Specific productivity_target value (m³/h or ton/h) and evaluation period.
Representative operations scenarios and acceptance criteria.
Safety compliance criteria and incident thresholds.
Network SLAs (latency, bandwidth, availability) for teleoperation.
Vehicle performance specifications (cycle time, load capacity, fuel efficiency).
Teleoperation interface design and operator training plan.
Safety policy definitions and enforcement mechanisms.
Telemetry data model and productivity calculation logic.
Integration test plan and verification strategy for the productivity KPI.
